complexity.summary: trivial: 553 chars, 0 files, 1 source lines, 0 hunks, 0 requirements, 0 modules

findings.summary: New input/output char caps are silently disabled by default, regressing prior safety behavior
findings.evidence: run_role() now always forwards max_input_chars=None, max_output_chars=None down to adversarial_common.runner.run_cli (scripts/phases/__init__.py:77-80), which is called from scripts/adversarial_plan.py:264-269,280-286,306-311,317-323 using args.max_input_chars/args.max_output_chars — both argparse flags default to None (scripts/adversarial_plan.py:396-399). runner.run_cli's own signature default is DEFAULT_MAX_INPUT_CHARS=256*1024 / DEFAULT_MAX_OUTPUT_CHARS=128*1024, but _optional_cap(name, value) in adversarial_common/runner.py returns None (i.e. 'no cap') whenever the caller explicitly passes None. Before this diff, run_role() did not forward these kwargs at all, so run_cli's built-in 256KB/128KB caps were always active. After this diff, unless a user explicitly passes --max-input-chars/--max-output-chars, every phase call now runs completely uncapped by default — the opposite of what R3/R4 (context/cost gating) are supposed to add. No test in this diff (tests/test_phases.py only asserts **kwargs are accepted, not their values) exercises this path.

findings.summary: New pre-flight spec gate requires a literal 'Requirements' heading, rejecting otherwise-valid specs
findings.evidence: ctx = gates.check_context("spec", spec_text) at scripts/adversarial_plan.py:234 uses the 'spec' threshold profile in adversarial_common/gates.py:20, which sets required_sections=["Requirements"]. check_context (gates.py:57-63) fails the whole run with EXIT_USAGE unless the spec contains a heading/line whose casefolded text is exactly 'Requirements' (not a substring match, e.g. 'Functional Requirements', 'Acceptance Criteria', or 'Goals' would NOT match). This is a new hard gate on real user-supplied spec.md files (adversarial_plan.py's stated purpose per its module docstring is to plan from an arbitrary spec.md); any spec that doesn't happen to use that exact heading name is now rejected before git setup even runs, even though it previously worked. The only reason existing tests still pass is that tests/conftest.py's VALID_SPEC fixture happens to already contain a '## Requirements' heading (conftest.py:29) — that fixture predates this diff and was not written to validate this new gate.

findings.summary: Added .gitignore entry '.adversarial-loop/' does not correspond to any path used in this repo
findings.evidence: The new line 7 adds '.adversarial-loop/' but the only artifacts directory this codebase ever writes to or ignores is '.adversarial-plan/' (scripts/adversarial_plan.py:158 gitops.ensure_gitignore(workdir, '.adversarial-plan/'); scripts/adversarial_plan.py:389 default --out '.adversarial-plan'; .gitignore line 1 already has '.adversarial-plan/'). A repo-wide grep for 'adversarial-loop' finds no other reference in this skill, suggesting a copy/paste from a sibling project (adversarial-code-loop) rather than an intentional addition.
